title: Three Growth Paths

content.statement: Growth can be funded through profits, outside capital, or employee ownership structures, and each path changes control, risk, and upside.
content.question: Which growth path best protects your control, speed, and long-term upside?
content.equation: Growth Capacity = Available Capital × Control Preference × Risk Tolerance
content.mental_model: Three doors, each opens to a different version of the future company.
content.business_lens: Choose whether to self-fund, raise equity, borrow, or involve employees based on the kind of company you want to build.
content.life_lens: Decide whether you want slower independence, faster shared ownership, or leveraged risk.
sop: Estimate how much capital growth requires
Decide how much control you want to keep
Compare self-funding, outside capital, and employee ownership options
Choose the path that matches your long-term vision
content.explainer: There are usually three broad ways to fund growth. You can use your own profits, which protects ownership but may slow you down. You can take outside capital, which may accelerate growth but gives others a claim on the future. Or you can involve employees through ownership-like programs.
questions: Which growth path best protects your control, speed, and long-term upside?
Would you rather grow three times slower and own 100%, or three times faster and own 60%?
What would you have to give up, equity, time, or debt risk, to fund the next stage of growth?

definitions.def: Giving up a slice of ownership so each existing owner's percentage shrinks — usually the result of issuing new shares to raise money. More investors means a smaller ownership stake for you.

content.equation_example: Rate each 1-10. Available Capital 4, Control Preference 9, Risk Tolerance 3. Score = 4 times 9 times 3 = 108 out of a possible 1,000. Higher means you have real options for how you fund growth. Lower means one path, usually the slowest, is your only realistic choice.
content.closing_statement: Every funding choice trades away something else.

caption:
Principle Nº 255: Three Growth Paths

Growth can be funded through profits, outside capital, or employee ownership structures, and each path changes control, risk, and upside. There are usually three broad ways to fund growth.

Ask yourself: Which growth path best protects your control, speed, and long-term upside?
